subject: grammar
title: Reporting Verbs Transformation
instruction: Report each of the sentences below using a verb from the list: agree, apologise, ask, deny, encourage, persuaded, promise, refuse, suggest. Remember that you do not need to report the original words exactly.

questions.stem: 'We are going to lower taxes and reduce unemployment!'
questions.answer: promised to lower taxes and reduce unemployment

questions.stem: 'I really think you should apply for the job. You'd have a good chance of getting it.'
questions.answer: encouraged me to apply for the job

questions.stem: 'No, I won't help you. Do it yourself!'
'Oh, okay, I'll go to the meeting.'
questions.answer: agreed to go to the meeting

questions.stem: 'I didn't cause the accident. It wasn't me.'
questions.answer: denied causing the accident|||denied having caused the accident|||denied that he had caused the accident

questions.stem: 'We are very sorry that we lost your application form.'
questions.answer: apologised for losing my application form

questions.stem: 'Why aren't there many poisonous snakes in Britain?'
questions.answer: asked why there aren't many poisonous snakes in Britain|||asked why there weren't many poisonous snakes in Britain

questions.stem: 'Are you going to the lecture tomorrow?'
questions.answer: asked if I was going to the lecture the next day|||asked whether I was going to the lecture the next day|||asked if I'm going to the lecture tomorrow

questions.stem: 'Why don't you have a day off? You could do with a rest.'
questions.answer: suggested that I have a day off|||suggested I have a day off|||suggested that I had a day off|||suggested I had a day off

questions.stem: 'Please come to the theatre with me, Dan. I really think you'll enjoy it.' 'Oh, alright then, Claire.'
questions.answer: persuaded Dan to go to the theatre with her|||persuaded Dan to come to the theatre with her
